Practice Management

Best online scheduling tools for therapists

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

# Best online scheduling tools for therapists You know the drill: a client emails at 11 pm asking for “any time next week,” you reply with three open slots, they pick one that’s already taken, and the ping-pong costs you two days. You just want a clean, HIPAA-safe way for clients to book themselves without the back-and-forth. This post walks you through the two online scheduling tools therapists actually use—SimplePractice and Calendly—so you can pick the one that matches how (and how much) you work. > **Quick verdict:** SimplePractice is the all-in-one choice for therapists who want scheduling plus notes, billing and telehealth in a HIPAA system. Calendly is best for low-cost, lightweight booking that plugs into the tools you already use. ## SimplePractice ### Pricing - Starter: $29/mo - Essential: $69/mo - Plus: $99/mo - 30-day free trial on all paid tiers; no free forever plan ### Stand-out features - Unlimited appointments on every paid plan - HIPAA-compliant scheduling with intake forms and consent boxes - Native telehealth and insurance billing—no extra logins - iOS/Android mobile app lets you approve or block requests between sessions ### Pros - Full EHR so you don’t need to duct-tape three apps together - Automated SMS/email reminders included; clients can confirm or cancel with one click - Client portal shows only the slots you want to offer, eliminating double-bookings - Insurance claims and ERA auto-posting save weekend admin time ### Cons - Price jumps quickly if you only need scheduling - Dashboard can feel busy until you hide the modules you don’t use - No free tier—after 30 days you pay or leave ### Best for Therapists who want scheduling, notes, billing and telehealth in one HIPAA system. ## Calendly ### Pricing - Basic: free (1 calendar, 1 event type) - Essentials: $10/mo (1 seat) - Professional: $15/mo (1 seat) - Teams: $20/mo (1 seat) - 14-day trial on paid tiers; downgrade to free anytime ### Stand-out features - 2-way sync with Google, Outlook, iCloud calendars - HIPAA compliance and BAA available on any paid plan - Group events, classes, round-robin if you ever run workshops - Workflows let you attach intake forms, send reminders, or tag in Mailchimp ### Pros - Live in five minutes—paste the link on your website or email signature - Free tier keeps you running until private-pay caseload grows - Integrates with Zoom, Stripe, Google Meet—pay and meet links auto-create - Time-zone intelligent so international clients don’t book 3 am slots ### Cons - No clinical notes, treatment plans, or billing—just scheduling - HIPAA toggle is off by default; you must request BAA on paid plans - Lower tiers limit branding and number of event types ### Best for Therapists who already use an EHR and only need lightweight, low-cost scheduling. ## Head-to-head comparison | Feature | SimplePractice | Calendly | |---|---|---| | Base monthly cost (solo) | $29 | $0 | | HIPAA compliance | Included on all paid tiers | Available on paid plans with BAA | | Calendar sync | 2-way with Google, iCal | 2-way with Google, Outlook, iCloud | | Client self-scheduling | Yes via secure portal | Yes via shareable link | | Intake forms at booking | Built-in | Via workflows (paid) | | Payment collection | Stripe integration built-in | Stripe or PayPal via workflows | | Clinical notes | Full EHR | None | | Free tier | No | Yes | ## Who should choose which Pick **SimplePractice** if you’re opening a solo practice and want one login for scheduling, notes, insurance billing, and telehealth. The higher price is still cheaper than paying for four separate tools, and you won’t outgrow it when you add a second clinician later. Pick **Calendly** if you already have an EHR you like and just need a friction-free booking link. Start on the free tier, then upgrade to Essentials ($10) the moment you need HIPAA protection or automated reminders. ## FAQ ### Is online scheduling HIPAA compliant for therapists? Yes, if the vendor signs a Business Associate Agreement. SimplePractice includes HIPAA on every paid plan; Calendly offers it on Essentials and above once you request the BAA. ### Can clients book themselves and still keep my calendar private? Absolutely. Both tools let you set “available windows” so clients see only the slots you choose to offer; the rest of your calendar stays invisible. ### Do these tools send automatic reminders to reduce no-shows? Yes. SimplePractice sends SMS/email reminders by default. Calendly does the same via Workflows once you’re on a paid plan. ### Will therapy appointment scheduling software sync with Google Calendar? Yes. Both platforms offer 2-way sync with Google, and Calendly also supports Outlook and iCloud. ### Can I collect intake forms or payments at booking? SimplePractice bundles intake forms and card-on-file in every plan. Calendly can attach Stripe payments and intake forms through its Workflows feature on paid tiers. ## Conclusion If you’re tired of email tag and want the simplest path to HIPAA-safe bookings, start with Calendly’s free tier and upgrade when you need reminders. If you’d rather replace your patchwork of notes, billing, and telehealth with one monthly bill, grab SimplePractice’s 30-day trial and import your client list in under an hour. Ready to stop the scheduling ping-pong? [Start your free SimplePractice trial here]Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free → or [set up Calendly in the next five minutes]Try CALENDLY free →.
